concept: Visit / Spend Bounded Time as a Guest
description: To spend a bounded period with a person or at a place as a guest. Departure is still part of the understood visit. The reason for coming belongs in the surrounding clause rather than the verb. 'menui' (dwell) makes a continuing home in a place; 'phemiru' crosses the threshold for a time.
sound_symbolism: Bilabial breath in 'phe' meets the stressed lip closure and bright nasal vowel of 'mi'. Before the last turn, 'phemi' (guest) sounds in full. Final 'ru' releases the tongue in a roll and drops into deep 'u'. A compact center leaves in a low flowing motion.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha womu phemiru.' (I visit this home.) The object may be a person or place, and time phrases can give the visit its duration. The event-noun rule supplies a visit.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A visit places company inside a chosen interval. Welcome and departure both stay available to the people sharing the space.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Visits carried news, labor, food, and kinship along paths where guest and host might reverse roles on the next journey.

tags.activity: spending a bounded time with a person or at a place
tags.community: company held through the guest relation
tags.temporal: a stay with an understood end
